arguments.task: Research Lark (Larksuite) on official and first-party pages only. Starting at https://open.larksuite.com, find and extract evidence for: 1) one-line product description, 2) developer authentication methods for public APIs, 3) whether API access is self-serve free, trial, paid, admin approval, or partner/contact-sales gated and why, 4) API surface type (REST, GraphQL, or both) and breadth, 5) official rate limit documentation/notes, 6) whether an official or clearly existing MCP server/integration exists, preferably from first-party docs or official GitHub/MCP registry pages, 7) any notable signals such as changelog/versioning/SDKs. Prefer official docs, auth docs, pricing/access pages, changelogs, GitHub repos, and support docs. Open relevant pages and return a concise evidence-backed summary with exact URLs visited.

input.thought: Start browser-based research on official Lark pages and related first-party evidence sources.

raw_result.description: Lark is an enterprise collaboration suite that combines messaging, meetings, docs, email, calendar, and workflow tools in one platform.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official developer docs describe app access token, tenant access token, and user access token flows for API access; OAuth is supported for user authorization, but the exact split between OAuth grant types and token exchange details was not fully verified in the gathered evidence.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public developer portal and documentation are openly accessible, and pricing evidence indicates a free Starter plan with Lark Developer included; however, whether every API capability is available on free tiers was not fully verified.

api_surface.breadth: Broad server APIs across multiple product areas including messaging/IM, docs, drive, calendar, base, approval, contacts, and other collaboration domains; no verified GraphQL evidence was found in the gathered official docs.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP server or clearly verified existing MCP listing was confirmed from the gathered evidence; absence is uncertain rather than definitive.

raw_result.blocker: Some access-tier and MCP-status details remain unverified from the gathered evidence, so a toolkit appears buildable from public REST docs and token auth but may need manual confirmation of plan entitlements and endpoint availability.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official frequency-control docs indicate HTTP 429 behavior and rate-limit headers including x-ogw-ratelimit information, with limits varying by app, tenant, and API.
raw_result.notable_signals: Active public changelog, broad product-domain API coverage, and visible developer platform/docs suggest a mature integration surface.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The gathered evidence supports public docs, token-based auth, REST APIs, and rate limits, but does not fully verify whether API access is universally available on free tiers or whether any official/non-official MCP server exists. OAuth details were also only partially confirmed.
